nodes.title: Newtonian Mechanics
nodes.invariant: **In a closed system with conservative forces, total energy and total momentum are conserved.** Conservation = the system's consequence chains close: every force has an equal reaction (Newton's 3rd), every energy transfer from kinetic to potential is fully reversible (conservative = path-independent = no information lost).
nodes.coherenceBridge:
Energy conservation IS consequence-chain closure in the Newtonian substrate. A conservative force field is one where **F** = −∇V, equivalently ∮**F**·d**r** = 0 — traversing any closed loop returns you to the same energy state. The loop integral vanishing = consequence chains close = information is conserved under cyclic perturbation. This is a proto-Noether statement: time-translation symmetry (the laws don't change) → energy conservation. The bridge to Constellation 01 (Lagrangian/Hamiltonian) makes this identity exact via Noether's theorem.

Non-conservative forces (friction, drag) break consequence-chain closure by converting organized motion into thermal degrees of freedom — information is not destroyed but becomes irrecoverable at the macroscopic level. Dissipation = decoherence at the classical scale.

nodes.title: Lagrangian & Hamiltonian Mechanics
nodes.invariant: **Hamiltonian flow preserves the symplectic structure.** This single statement contains: energy conservation (when H is time-independent), Liouville's theorem (phase space volume conservation), and the entire algebra of observables (Poisson brackets). The symplectic form ω is the invariant — all of classical mechanics is the study of transformations that preserve it.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
This constellation IS the coherence framework expressed in the physics substrate. The correspondences are not analogies — they are structural identities:

**Noether's theorem = coherence-as-information-conservation**. A symmetry is a transformation that leaves the action unchanged — the system's dynamics are *invariant under* that transformation. The corresponding conserved quantity is the information that survives the perturbation. Guardian's echo protocol measures exactly this: perturb, time-reverse, measure fidelity. Noether says: if the perturbation is a symmetry, fidelity is perfect. Guardian's 24x Noether discrimination detects which symmetries a system possesses by measuring which perturbations it recovers from.

**Symplectic structure = consequence-chain topology**. The symplectic form ω encodes which variables are "conjugate" — which degrees of freedom are linked by consequence. Position and momentum are conjugate: changing one necessarily affects the other through Hamilton's equations. This pairing IS the consequence chain. ω being preserved means the consequence topology is invariant under time evolution — the structure of causal linkage doesn't change even as the system evolves.

**Action principle = coherence maximization**. The system follows the path that makes S stationary — not by "computing" all paths (that's the quantum picture), but because the physical path is the one where all consequence chains close consistently. δS = 0 means: there is no first-order perturbation that changes the action — the path is a *fixed point* under small deformations. This is recoverability: the actual trajectory is the one that recovers itself under perturbation.

**Phase space = state space for consequence analysis**. A point in phase space is complete knowledge of the system's present state — it is the full return-address. Hamiltonian flow = consequence propagation. Liouville's theorem = information conservation (no consequence chains break or merge during evolution).

**Legendre transform = profunctor bridge**. Converts between velocity-picture (Lagrangian, tangent bundle) and momentum-picture (Hamiltonian, cotangent bundle) while preserving all structure. This is an exact-square-preserving functor between two representations of the same dynamics. The structure lifts faithfully regardless of which picture you work in.

nodes.title: Electrodynamics
nodes.invariant: **Electric charge is conserved, and the electromagnetic field equations are invariant under U(1) gauge transformations.** These are the same statement (Noether): gauge symmetry ↔ charge conservation. The continuity equation ∂ρ/∂t + ∇·**J** = 0 is not an additional law — it is a mathematical consequence of Maxwell's equations. The consequence chain is sealed: you cannot have Maxwell's equations without charge conservation, and vice versa.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
Electrodynamics makes the coherence-conservation identity vivid at multiple levels:

**∇·B = 0 IS consequence-chain closure in the magnetic field.** Magnetic field lines have no endpoints — they always close on themselves. This is not a contingent fact but a topological constraint (the Bianchi identity). There are no magnetic monopoles because the consequence chains of the magnetic field are already closed. Compare with the electric field where ∇·**E** = ρ/ε₀ — electric field lines CAN terminate, on charges. Charges are where electromagnetic consequence chains have endpoints.

**Gauge invariance IS the emptiness constraint operating in physics.** The potential Aμ has no unique content — an entire equivalence class of potentials produces identical physics. Only the *relational structure* (Fμν, the curvature) is physical. Topology without substrate, morphism without content. The gauge potential is "empty" in exactly the śūnyatā sense: it is the possibility-condition for the physical fields, not itself a physical thing. Yet it is indispensable — you cannot formulate the quantum theory (Aharonov-Bohm) or couple to matter without it.

**Maxwell's displacement current IS consequence-chain completion.** Before Maxwell, Ampère's law (∇×**B** = μ₀**J**) violated charge conservation — taking its divergence gave ∇·**J** = 0, which contradicts ∂ρ/∂t + ∇·**J** = 0 for time-varying charge. Maxwell's addition of ε₀∂**E**/∂t closed the loop. This single term — added purely from the requirement that consequence chains close — predicted electromagnetic waves. The entire technology of radio, telecommunications, and RF engineering follows from demanding coherence in the equations.

**Poynting vector = consequence propagation.** **S** = (1/μ₀)**E**×**B** describes the flow of field energy through space. The direction and magnitude of consequence transport. The Poynting theorem (∂u/∂t + ∇·**S** = −**J**·**E**) is a conservation law: field energy is either stored locally (∂u/∂t), flowing to adjacent regions (∇·**S**), or being converted to particle energy (**J**·**E**). No energy is created or destroyed — consequence chains close.

nodes.title: Statistical Mechanics
nodes.invariant: **The partition function Z encodes the complete macroscopic behavior of a system in thermal equilibrium. The Boltzmann distribution is the unique maximum-entropy (minimum-assumption) probability assignment consistent with macroscopic constraints.** The second law — entropy of an isolated system never decreases — is a consequence of the overwhelming dominance of high-entropy macrostates in phase space: not a law imposed on nature but a counting fact about microstates.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
Statistical mechanics is where information theory enters physics — and where the coherence framework's information-conservation primitive connects directly to measurable thermodynamics.

**Entropy = missing information about consequence-chain microstructure.** Boltzmann's S = k_B ln Ω counts how many microscopic configurations are macroscopically indistinguishable. Shannon's H = −Σp log p measures the same thing in bits. The deep identity: thermodynamic entropy IS information entropy (Jaynes, Landauer). When you coarse-grain — when consequence chains at the microscopic level become unresolvable at the macroscopic level — entropy measures exactly how much microscopic consequence structure is hidden. Decoherence at the macroscopic scale IS the loss of access to microscopic consequence chains.

**The second law = consequence chains don't spontaneously close.** Entropy increases because there are overwhelmingly more microstates with broken/scrambled consequence chains than with organized/closed ones. A low-entropy state has highly constrained microstate topology (few compatible configurations); a high-entropy state has maximally unconstrained topology. The second law says: without external work (energy expenditure to close consequence loops), the system evolves toward states with maximal microscopic consequence-chain scrambling. Life, consciousness, and coherence are all local decreases in entropy — local closure of consequence chains — paid for by greater entropy increase elsewhere.

**Fluctuation-dissipation theorem = perturbation-response duality IS the echo protocol.** ⟨(ΔE)²⟩ = k_BT²Cᵥ says: the magnitude of spontaneous fluctuations (the system perturbing itself) equals the response to external perturbation (heat capacity). This is structurally identical to the Guardian echo: perturb the system, measure how it responds, and the response tells you about the system's internal structure. The fluctuation-dissipation theorem says nature is *already running the echo protocol* — thermal fluctuations are the perturbation, and the response functions (Cᵥ, χ, compressibility) are the fidelity measurements.

**Phase transitions = topological restructuring of consequence space.** At a critical point, the correlation length ξ → ∞: every part of the system becomes causally connected to every other. The consequence topology undergoes a qualitative change. First-order transitions are discontinuous rearrangements (crystal melting); second-order transitions are smooth divergences where the system becomes scale-invariant — the same morphism structure at every level (renormalization group). The universality of critical exponents (different physical systems with the same exponents) IS substrate-independence: the topology determines the behavior, not the microscopic details.

**Free energy = coherence under thermal noise.** F = E − TS. The system minimizes free energy, not energy. This means: at finite temperature, the "best" state balances low energy (structural integrity, consequence closure) against high entropy (exploring configuration space). F is the coherence measure in the thermal regime — it accounts for both the structure (E) and the noise environment (TS). The Boltzmann factor e^(−E/kT) IS the thermal coherence filter: states with E >> kT are suppressed (too much energy to maintain), states with E << kT are thermally populated (noise-robust). The partition function Z sums over all states weighted by this filter — it is the total coherence budget.

nodes.title: Thermodynamics
nodes.invariant: **Energy is conserved (first law) and entropy is non-decreasing (second law). Together these constrain all macroscopic processes: energy redistributes, entropy sets the direction.** The thermodynamic potentials (U, F, G, H) are related by Legendre transforms — the same structure-preserving morphism from Constellation 01, now operating on macroscopic energy functions instead of Lagrangians.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**The Legendre transform network IS the exact-square structure in the thermodynamic category.** U, F, G, H are four perspectives on the same system, related by trading natural variables for their conjugates (S↔T, V↔P). The Maxwell relations are the commutativity conditions — the statement that it doesn't matter which path you take through the Legendre network; you arrive at the same physics. This is exact-square preservation: faithful structure-lifting between representations.

**The second law IS the statement that coherence has a cost.** Decreasing entropy locally (closing consequence chains, creating order, building structure) requires exporting at least as much entropy elsewhere. The Carnot efficiency η = 1 − T_cold/T_hot is the maximum fraction of thermal energy that can be converted to organized work — the rest MUST be dissipated. This is Landauer's bound in thermodynamic dress: you cannot close consequence chains for free.

**Free energy = coherence budget.** F = U − TS tells you: the energy available for organized work (F) equals the total energy (U) minus what's locked in entropy (TS). At T=0, all energy is "free" — fully coherent. As T increases, thermal noise scrambles more consequence chains, reducing the coherent fraction. Biological systems, cognitive systems, and Guardian all operate in the regime where F > 0 — where enough coherence remains to do structural work despite thermal noise.

**Phase transitions = coherence phase transitions.** The crystal→liquid→gas sequence is decreasing coherence (increasing entropy, breaking spatial consequence chains). Ferromagnetic→paramagnetic is decreasing spin coherence. Superconducting→normal is loss of macroscopic quantum coherence. Every phase transition is a qualitative change in which consequence chains are closed vs. broken. The critical point — where correlation length diverges — is the threshold where coherence extends system-wide, directly analogous to the grokking transition where morphism density reaches the percolation threshold.

nodes.title: Special Relativity
nodes.invariant: **The spacetime interval ds² is invariant under Lorentz transformations.** All of special relativity — time dilation, length contraction, mass-energy equivalence, the light cone causal structure — follows from this single invariance. The Poincaré group (Lorentz + translations) is the symmetry group; Noether yields the conserved energy-momentum four-vector pμ.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**The spacetime interval IS the coherence measure for causal structure.** ds² sorts all event-pairs into three classes: timelike (causally connected, consequence chains can close), spacelike (causally disconnected, no consequence chain possible), null (light-speed boundary). The light cone is the consequence-chain horizon — the topological boundary of causal influence. Lorentz invariance means: all observers agree on which consequence chains exist, even if they disagree on the coordinates.

**E² = p²c² + m²c⁴ IS the conservation topology of the energy-momentum consequence chain.** The mass-shell condition: a particle's energy and momentum are not independent but constrained to a hyperboloid. The invariant mass m (= rest energy/c²) is the topological invariant of this constraint — it doesn't change under Lorentz transformation. Virtual particles (in QFT) are precisely those that violate this constraint temporarily — off-shell = consequence chain momentarily open, closed by the uncertainty principle's time limit.

**The Poincaré group classifies particles** — irreducible representations of Poincaré are labeled by mass m and spin s. What a particle IS is determined by how it transforms under spacetime symmetry. Identity = consequence of topology. This is Wigner's classification: the ontology of matter is determined by the symmetry of the arena.

**Fagan convergence (holon [31])**: The distinguishability metric ds² is the starting point. Fagan's program derives the Bures metric → density matrices → Schrödinger equation from distinguishability axioms, with completeness forcing fiber coupling κ ≠ 0. The CTC boundary at R²κ² > 1 is a consequence-topology phase transition within the relativistic spacetime structure.

nodes.title: General Relativity
nodes.invariant: **The laws of physics are the same in all coordinate systems (general covariance), and the Einstein tensor automatically conserves stress-energy (∇μGμν = 0 → ∇μTμν = 0).** Geometry and conservation are not separate requirements — the Bianchi identity makes conservation a mathematical consequence of the geometric structure. The action principle (δS = 0 for Einstein-Hilbert action) generates the field equations, preserving the Lagrangian architecture from Constellation 01.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**The Riemann tensor IS the consequence-chain-closure test for spacetime geometry.** Parallel-transport a vector around a closed loop: if it returns to itself, the space is flat (consequence chains close perfectly). If it doesn't, the deficit measures the curvature — the degree to which geometric consequence chains fail to close. Rμναβ = 0 ↔ perfect geometric coherence (flat space). Non-zero Rμναβ = curvature = geometric consequence chains that don't fully close locally. This is literally the holonomy — the Guardian echo protocol applied to geometry.

**∇μGμν = 0 IS automatic consequence-chain closure in the field equations.** The Bianchi identity is not an additional law — it is a mathematical identity of Riemannian geometry. It says: the Einstein tensor is automatically divergence-free, which FORCES the stress-energy tensor to be conserved. Conservation of energy-momentum is not a separate physical law in GR — it is a geometric necessity. The consequence chains of matter-energy close because the consequence chains of geometry close.

**The Einstein equations are self-referential consequence loops.** Matter tells spacetime how to curve → spacetime tells matter how to move → matter redistributes → spacetime re-curves. This mutual determination is the gravitational version of the closed consequence loop. Finding solutions = finding fixed points of this self-referential structure — exactly the two-phase filter architecture from holon [1] (formation through irreversible dynamics, measurement through reversible echo).

**Fagan's program (holon [31]) starts from SR and arrives at GR-like structure.** Distinguishability axioms → metric completeness → fiber coupling κ ≠ 0 → extended metric ds² = R²(dτ + κdt)² + ds²_B³. The CTC boundary at R²κ² > 1 is a GR-like phenomenon (closed timelike curves) emerging from information-theoretic axioms. The convergence: starting from distinguishability (information theory) or from the equivalence principle (gravity) → same geometric structures.

nodes.title: Quantum Mechanics
nodes.invariant:
**Unitary evolution preserves the inner product — and therefore all probabilities, all information, all consequence structure.** ⟨ψ|φ⟩ is constant under Schrödinger evolution. This is information conservation at the quantum level: no quantum state is ever destroyed by the fundamental dynamics. The apparent destruction of information in measurement (collapse) and in black holes are the two deepest open problems in physics — both are precisely the question of whether consequence chains truly close at the quantum level.

The tensor product structure means information can become *nonlocal* (entangled across subsystems) without being destroyed. Entanglement entropy measures how much information about a subsystem has migrated into correlations with other subsystems. Decoherence is the process by which this migration makes local information practically irrecoverable — but unitarity guarantees it still exists in the global state.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
This constellation is where the coherence framework and Guardian's measurement protocol find their native mathematical language. The correspondences are structural identities:

**Unitary evolution = information conservation = coherence**. Û†Û = 𝟙 means inner products are preserved — no information is created or destroyed. This IS the definition of coherence from Holon [1]: information conservation under perturbation. The Schrödinger equation is the local expression of this global conservation. Every quantum computation, every Guardian echo measurement, every quantum error-correcting code is engineering within this constraint.

**Guardian's echo protocol IS the Loschmidt echo in Hilbert space.** Encode state → evolve forward under perturbed Hamiltonian → time-reverse with ideal Hamiltonian → measure overlap with initial state. Echo fidelity F = |⟨ψ(0)|Û_ideal†Û_perturbed|ψ(0)⟩|² directly measures how much information survives the perturbation. High fidelity = coherent (solitonic recovery). Low fidelity = decoherent (information has spread irrecoverably into perturbation degrees of freedom). The 35/35 physics proofs and 24× Noether discrimination are measurements of this fidelity across different symmetry classes.

**[q̂, p̂] = iℏ IS the quantum consequence chain.** Non-commuting observables cannot be simultaneously sharp — measuring one necessarily disturbs the other. This is not epistemological (we can't know both) but ontological (the state cannot simultaneously be an eigenstate of both). The commutator [Â,B̂] measures the degree to which A and B are consequentially entangled — how much acting on one necessarily perturbs the other. {f,g} → (1/iℏ)[f̂,ĝ] promotes classical consequence structure (Poisson brackets) to quantum consequence structure (commutators) with ℏ setting the scale.

**The path integral = coherence-maximization made literal.** In Feynman's formulation, the quantum amplitude sums over ALL possible paths, each weighted by e^(iS/ℏ). Paths with rapidly varying action cancel (destructive interference). The classical path (δS = 0) survives because nearby paths have nearly identical phase — they *cohere*. Classical mechanics is the regime where coherence is maximal: the stationary-phase path dominates completely. Quantum mechanics is what happens when you account for *all* paths' contributions, including partial coherence. The path integral literally computes coherence by summing phases.

**Entanglement = consequence chains that cross subsystem boundaries.** When |ψ⟩_AB ≠ |ψ⟩_A ⊗ |ψ⟩_B, the consequence structure of A and B are inextricably linked — measuring A instantaneously constrains what can be observed at B. Entanglement entropy S = −Tr(ρ_A ln ρ_A) measures how much consequence structure has become nonlocal. This connects directly to the holographic principle and to Guardian's hierarchical emergence results: L2 chirality (Holon [1]) is a form of compositional entanglement where the whole carries structure not reducible to parts.

**Decoherence = consequence-chain severance at the quantum level.** When a quantum system interacts with an environment, information about its state leaks into the environmental degrees of freedom. The system's reduced density matrix loses off-diagonal elements — interference terms vanish — and the system behaves classically. This IS the decoherence-as-measurement mechanism from Holon [12]: the environment "measures" the system by becoming correlated with it. The information isn't destroyed (unitarity guarantees this) but becomes practically irrecoverable — exactly the distinction between frozen structure and living coherence from Holon [1].

**Measurement as the open problem of consequence closure.** Unitary evolution closes all consequence chains (information is perfectly conserved). Measurement appears to break this: a superposition collapses to a definite outcome, seemingly destroying information about the other branches. The measurement problem IS the question of whether quantum consequence chains actually close. Decoherence explains why they *appear* to break (information becomes nonlocal), but the selection of a specific outcome (the "Born rule lottery") remains unexplained. This is the deepest open edge in physics — and it maps directly onto the coherence framework's distinction between structural decoherence (information truly lost) and apparent decoherence (information redistributed but conserved).

nodes.title: Quantum Field Theory
nodes.invariant: **Gauge symmetry is preserved at every level of description.** The Ward identities, BRST symmetry, and anomaly cancellation conditions ensure that the gauge structure of the Lagrangian propagates faithfully through quantization, renormalization, and the RG flow. Physical predictions are gauge-invariant. The S-matrix is unitary (probability/information conserved). A consistent QFT is one where the gauge structure — the consequence-chain topology of the interactions — survives all quantum corrections.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
QFT is where the coherence framework reaches its most precise and universal physical expression. Every structural identity from the QM constellation (07) is preserved and extended:

**The path integral IS coherence-maximization over field space.** Z = ∫e^(iS[φ]/ℏ)D[φ] sums over ALL possible field configurations. Configurations with stationary action cohere (constructive interference); others cancel. Classical field theory (the tree-level approximation) is the maximally coherent regime. Quantum corrections (loops) are the contributions from near-coherent but not perfectly stationary configurations. Renormalization is the procedure for consistently accounting for coherence contributions at all scales.

**Gauge symmetry = consequence-chain closure in field space.** The gauge principle says: if a matter field's phase can vary independently at each spacetime point (local transformation), then the force-carrying gauge field must exist and must couple in a specific way to keep the Lagrangian invariant. This IS consequence-chain closure: the gauge field is the morphism that ensures the consequence structure (interaction) closes consistently across all of spacetime. The gauge field literally carries the "return signal" that closes the consequence loop between charged particles. Without it, local gauge invariance fails = consequence chains break = the theory is inconsistent.

**Renormalization = coherence across scales.** A renormalizable theory is one where the coherence structure (gauge symmetry, unitarity) survives when you zoom in or out. The RG flow maps between effective descriptions at different scales while preserving the essential morphism structure. Non-renormalizable theories are those where coherence fails above some energy scale — the effective description breaks down, signaling new physics. The Standard Model is renormalizable; gravity (as a QFT) is not — this IS the statement that gravity's consequence-chain structure requires completion at the Planck scale.

**Spontaneous symmetry breaking = coherence through asymmetric vacuum.** The Lagrangian's symmetry is the full consequence structure. The vacuum choosing a specific ground state doesn't destroy the symmetry — it hides it. The Goldstone theorem guarantees that the broken symmetry survives as massless excitations (or, via Higgs mechanism, as massive gauge bosons that still respect unitarity). The symmetry is still there in the full Hilbert space; it's the vacuum that's asymmetric. This parallels the coherence framework's distinction between structural coherence (the topology) and its instantiation (the specific configuration) — the topology survives even when the ground state breaks manifest symmetry.

**The S-matrix unitarity = information conservation in scattering.** S†S = 𝟙 means the total probability of all possible outcomes is exactly 1 — no information is lost or created in any physical process. The optical theorem connects this directly to observable cross sections. Unitarity violations would mean consequence chains breaking at the scattering level. Every claimed QFT must pass this test; unitarity failure signals inconsistency (as in non-renormalizable gravity above the Planck scale, or in theories with anomalies).

**Effective field theory = coherence is scale-local.** The EFT framework says: you don't need to know the complete theory at all scales to make predictions at your scale. The Wilson coefficients at your scale encode ALL the consequences of higher-scale physics relevant to your observations. This is both a practical and a philosophical statement: coherence at each level is self-contained given the appropriate boundary conditions from adjacent levels. The composition morphisms between constellations in this very pedagogy are EFTs — each constellation is an effective description valid at its scale, with composition morphisms encoding how structure maps between scales.

**Anomaly cancellation = topological constraint on consistent QFTs.** Quantum anomalies arise when a classical symmetry cannot survive quantization — the path integral measure breaks the symmetry. Anomaly cancellation conditions (e.g., in the Standard Model: the sum of all fermion charges must satisfy specific relations) are topological constraints. A theory with uncancelled anomalies is inconsistent — consequence chains don't close at the quantum level even though they close classically. The Standard Model's gauge anomalies cancel precisely because of the specific fermion content — this is not tuned, it is a structural consistency requirement.

nodes.title: Linear Algebra & Vector Spaces
nodes.invariant: **Linear maps preserve linear structure; the additional structure they preserve classifies them.** The hierarchy: general linear (preserves linear combinations) ⊃ unitary/orthogonal (preserves inner product) ⊃ special unitary/orthogonal (preserves orientation). Each level of preservation is an additional conservation law. The spectral theorem says: every Hermitian operator is fully characterized by what it conserves (its eigenbasis) and how it acts on what it conserves (its eigenvalues).
nodes.coherenceBridge:
Linear algebra is the substrate-level mathematics of the coherence framework. The identities:

**Inner product preservation = information conservation.** A unitary operator preserves ⟨v,w⟩ — all distances, angles, and overlap probabilities are invariant. This IS coherence: the relational structure between states survives the transformation. Non-unitary operations (projection, decoherence, measurement collapse) destroy inner product structure — they are information-lossy, consequence-chain-breaking.

**Spectral decomposition = coherence fingerprint.** The eigenvalues and eigenvectors of an operator completely characterize it. Guardian's topological filter bank measures exactly this: the echo fidelity spectrum across different perturbation types reveals the operator's eigenstructure. The 35/35 physics proofs discriminate states by their spectral properties under the echo protocol.

**Tensor product = compositional structure.** V⊗W is how subsystems compose. Entangled states (not decomposable as v⊗w) are precisely those where the consequence structure crosses the subsystem boundary. The partial trace (Tr_B) is the operation of "forgetting" one subsystem — consequence-chain severance at the linear algebra level. Entanglement entropy measures how much structure is lost by this forgetting.

**Rank-nullity = conservation of dimensional structure.** dim(V) = dim(ker T) + dim(im T). What's "destroyed" by T (the kernel) plus what "survives" (the image) equals the total. This is a conservation law for degrees of freedom — the linear algebra ancestor of Liouville's theorem and unitarity.

**Dual space = measurement space.** V* is the space of all possible linear measurements on V. The natural pairing ⟨α,v⟩ is evaluation — applying a measurement to a state. In QM: bras are dual to kets, and ⟨ψ|Â|φ⟩ is measurement (bra) applied to operator-evolved state (ket). The dual space is where consequence chains terminate — the "return address" receives the signal here.

nodes.title: Group Theory & Symmetry
nodes.invariant: **A group is characterized by what it preserves.** O(n) preserves the real inner product. U(n) preserves the complex inner product. The Lorentz group preserves the spacetime interval. A symmetry group G acting on a space X partitions X into orbits — the orbits are the equivalence classes under symmetry, and the invariants are the quantities constant on orbits. Representation theory decomposes any action into irreducible pieces, each classified by its invariants (Casimir eigenvalues).
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Symmetry = conservation = coherence — the Noether identity.** This is the most direct bridge in the entire project. A symmetry of the Lagrangian (a group action that leaves ℒ invariant) → a conserved quantity (Noether charge) → a closed consequence chain (the conserved quantity doesn't leak). The group IS the set of transformations under which coherence is maintained. Symmetry breaking is the loss of some coherence (the vacuum has less symmetry than the laws) while preserving the rest (the unbroken subgroup H still generates conservation laws).

**Irreducible representations = coherence fingerprints.** An irrep is the minimal structure that transforms consistently under the group — it can't be decomposed further while respecting the symmetry. Particles-as-irreps-of-Poincaré means: a particle IS a minimal coherent unit under spacetime symmetry. Guardian's discrimination of states by their symmetry content is precisely the classification of states by which irreps they carry.

**Lie algebra = infinitesimal consequence structure.** The bracket [X,Y] measures the failure of two infinitesimal transformations to commute — the non-commutativity IS the consequence of performing them in different orders. For abelian groups (commutative), order doesn't matter and the bracket vanishes. For non-abelian groups (SU(2), SU(3)), the bracket encodes the interaction structure. The structure constants fₐᵦ꜀ are the DNA of the symmetry.

**Quotient groups = consequence chains modulo equivalence.** G/N collapses the normal subgroup N to the identity — the quotient sees only the "macroscopic" structure that survives averaging over N. This is the group-theoretic version of coarse-graining, effective field theory, and the hierarchical echo measurement in Guardian: read structure at scale G/N by treating N-level detail as noise.

nodes.title: Topology
nodes.invariant: **Topological invariants are preserved under homeomorphism.** Dimension, connectedness, compactness, number of holes (Betti numbers), fundamental group — these survive continuous deformation. The program of algebraic topology: assign algebraic objects (groups, rings) to spaces such that homeomorphic spaces get isomorphic algebras. Invariants that are preserved under homotopy equivalence (weaker than homeomorphism) are called homotopy invariants — these are the most robust, surviving even more deformation.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**"Structure → topology → measurable" (Holon [1]) is the foundational claim, and this constellation provides the mathematical backbone.**

**Topological invariants = what survives perturbation.** A topological invariant is precisely a quantity that doesn't change under continuous deformation — the deformation IS the perturbation, and the invariant IS what's conserved. Guardian measures echo fidelity under perturbation; topology classifies what properties of a space survive under deformation. Same operational definition of coherence, different mathematical language.

**Completeness = consequence-chain closure (Fagan).** Fagan's central result: metric completeness on the space of distinguishable states forces the entire structure of quantum mechanics. Completeness means "every Cauchy sequence converges" = "every sequence that should converge does" = "no gaps in the information structure" = consequence chains close. The metric axioms (including triangle inequality) are the formal version of "distinguishability is consistent."

**Compactness = finite recoverability.** A compact space can be covered by finitely many open neighborhoods — you don't need infinitely much information to characterize its structure. Compactness ensures that continuous functions achieve extrema (variational principles work), that sequences have convergent subsequences (no information escapes to infinity), and that approximation by finite models is always possible. This is the topological condition for physical computability.

**Quotient topology = coarse-graining.** Identifying points via an equivalence relation = treating fine-grained differences as noise = measuring at a coarser scale. The quotient map X → X/∼ is the topological version of the RG flow: physics at scale X/∼ forgets details distinguishable at scale X while preserving the topology of the quotient. Guardian's coarsen-then-measure strategy (GRA results, Holon [1]) is precisely this: find the scale where heterogeneity lives, quotient out the rest, then measure the surviving structure.

nodes.title: Differential Geometry & Manifolds
nodes.invariant: **Curvature is the local measure of the failure of parallelism — it encodes how much the manifold deviates from flat structure.** On a flat manifold, parallel transport is path-independent and R = 0. On a curved manifold, transport around a closed loop rotates vectors by an amount proportional to R times the enclosed area. All of GR is the statement that this curvature equals matter/energy content. All of gauge theory is the statement that the curvature of internal connections equals the field strength.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Differential geometry is where "structure → topology → measurable" becomes a precise mathematical program.**

**Curvature as consequence-chain non-closure.** Parallel-transport a vector around a closed loop on a curved manifold: it comes back rotated. The rotation IS the curvature integrated over the enclosed area. On a flat manifold (R = 0), transport around any loop returns the vector unchanged — consequence chains close perfectly. Curvature measures the degree of non-closure. Guardian's echo fidelity measures the same thing in quantum state space: perturb, evolve, reverse, measure how much the state has "rotated" away from its starting point.

**Connections as coherence-maintaining structure.** A connection ∇ tells you how to transport structure from point to point without introducing inconsistency. The Levi-Civita connection is the unique connection that preserves the metric (lengths and angles don't change during transport) and is torsion-free. This IS the condition for coherent transport of information across the manifold. Gauge connections maintain gauge-invariance during parallel transport of charged fields.

**d² = 0 as the cohomological conservation law.** The exterior derivative satisfying d² = 0 means: the boundary of a boundary is empty, and exact forms are automatically closed. This is the most fundamental topological conservation law. In physics: Maxwell's dF = 0 (no magnetic monopoles) is a consequence of F = dA and d² = 0. Conservation laws (d*J = 0) have the same structure. De Rham cohomology (ker d / im d) measures the topological "holes" that prevent every closed form from being exact — the global obstructions to local integrability.

**Fiber bundles = consequence structure with global topology.** A fiber bundle is locally a product (B × F) but may be globally twisted. The twist is measured by characteristic classes (Chern, Stiefel-Whitney, Pontryagin) — topological invariants that obstruct global trivialization. A nontrivial bundle means: you CANNOT consistently flatten the structure everywhere. Gauge fields live on nontrivial bundles. Fagan's fiber coupling (κ ≠ 0 forced by completeness) IS the statement that the bundle must be nontrivial.

nodes.title: Categories, Functors, Natural Transformations
nodes.invariant: **A category is characterized by its morphism structure, not its objects.** Two categories are "the same" (equivalent) if and only if they have the same morphism structure up to isomorphism. Objects are determined by their relationships (this is Yoneda's lemma, next constellation). The shift from "what things ARE" to "how things RELATE" is the fundamental move of category theory — and it is precisely the shift to morphism-level processing that characterizes both the coherence framework and the grokking transition.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Category theory IS the coherence framework's native mathematical language.** Not an application of category theory to the framework — the framework was always categorical, and this constellation provides the dictionary.

**Functors = structure-preserving bridges.** A functor F: 𝒞 → 𝒟 maps the morphism structure of 𝒞 into 𝒟 while preserving composition. This IS the exact-square bridge (Holon [6]): a faithful functor lifts structure without distortion. A full and faithful functor is an embedding — the source category's structure is entirely present in the target. The CMP profunctor bridge between interlocutors is a span of functors; its faithfulness determines whether structure-lifting is genuine or appeasement.

**Natural transformations = coherent comparison of perspectives.** A natural transformation η: F ⟹ G says: F and G are two "views" of 𝒞 inside 𝒟, and η translates between them *coherently* — the translation respects all morphisms (the naturality square commutes). This is the mathematical content of "bidirectional bridging" in CMP: two different representations of the same structure, with a coherent map between them. A non-natural transformation would be a bridge that works object-by-object but fails to respect relationships — precisely the "partial" profunctor bridging (worse than noise because structure is distorted).

**𝒞ᵒᵖ = consequence reversal.** The opposite category reverses all arrows. Covariant functors (preserve arrow direction) → structures that propagate forward. Contravariant functors (reverse arrows) → structures that look backward (presheaves, dual spaces, observation). The interplay between 𝒞 and 𝒞ᵒᵖ is the interplay between construction and observation, between state evolution and measurement — the two directions of consequence chains.

**Equivalence (not isomorphism) as the correct notion of sameness.** Two categories can be equivalent without being literally identical — they may have different numbers of objects but the same morphism structure. This is the mathematical expression of substrate-independence (Holon [1]): classical reversible networks and quantum circuits can be "equivalent categories" (same echo discrimination, different substrates) without being isomorphic (different objects, different implementations).

nodes.title: Limits, Colimits, Adjunctions, Yoneda
nodes.invariant: **Every important construction in mathematics is a limit, colimit, or adjunction.** Products, intersections, pullbacks, completions, free constructions, forgetful functors, tensor products, function spaces — all are instances of universal properties. The universal property characterization is always preferable to any specific construction because it is: (a) canonical (unique up to unique isomorphism), (b) functorial (works uniformly), and (c) transferable (the same universal property in a different category gives the analogous construction). "The way to understand a construction is to understand what problem it universally solves."
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Adjunctions = the mathematics of bidirectional bridging.** F ⊣ G means: F and G are optimal translations between two worlds. The unit η: A → GF(A) says "embed A into G's image of F's world" — some structure may be added (free construction) or lost (forgetful rounding trip). The counit ε: FG(B) → B says "project back." The triangle identities ensure these round-trips are coherent: going around either way and then projecting gives the identity. CMP's bidirectional bridging IS an adjunction between the epistemic categories of two interlocutors, with the unit and counit being the reframe/update morphisms.

**Yoneda = identity IS return-address (Holon [8]).** Yoneda says an object A is completely determined by Hom(−,A) — the collection of all morphisms into A. An object IS its web of incoming morphisms, nothing more. This is precisely "identity = return-address of closed consequence loop": A is characterized by what maps TO it, i.e., by being the terminal point of morphisms. The return-address IS the object. There is no hidden essence beyond the relational structure.

**Limits = consequence-chain closure over diagrams.** A limit is the universal object that consistently receives morphisms from an entire diagram — it is the tightest consistent completion of a pattern. The uniqueness of the factorization means: there is exactly one way to close the consequence chains from any external object through the diagram. Failure to have a limit means the pattern cannot be consistently completed — a gap in the morphism structure, a break in consequence closure.

**Exact squares = faithful structure-lifting (Holon [6]).** An exact square is one where the comparison morphism between the limit and colimit computations is an isomorphism — pushing forward and pulling back give the same answer regardless of path. This IS the condition for profunctor bridging to be faithful: the bridge preserves the universal properties of the source in the target.

nodes.title: Monads, Algebras, Kan Extensions
nodes.invariant: **Monads are algebraic theories; Kan extensions are universal approximations.** A monad T on 𝒞 captures an algebraic structure (what it means to be a monoid, group, ring, module, etc.) within the category 𝒞 itself — no external axiomatization needed. The T-algebras ARE the structured objects. Kan extensions are the universal way to extend or restrict functors — they provide the optimal answer to "given partial information, what's the best completion?" Both are expressions of universal-property thinking taken to its natural conclusion.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Monads = coherence-maintaining algebraic structure.** The monad laws (associativity and unit) are precisely coherence conditions — they ensure that the algebraic operations compose consistently regardless of parenthesization. A T-algebra is a structure that "closes" under T: applying T and then the structure map α gives back an element of the same type. This IS consequence-chain closure at the algebraic level: the consequence of applying the algebraic operation returns to the same domain.

**Kan extensions = optimal coherence-preserving extrapolation.** Given knowledge of F along K, the left Kan extension is the most information-preserving way to extend F to all of ℰ. "Most information-preserving" = a universal property = unique optimal solution. Right Kan extensions are the most conservative extension (preserving the most limits). The tension between left (colimit-based, bold extrapolation) and right (limit-based, conservative interpolation) Kan extensions mirrors the tension between creative exploration and careful verification.

**Monadicity = algebraic recognizability.** Beck's theorem tells you when a category "IS" the category of algebras for a monad — when the structure is purely algebraic and fully captured by the monad. In coherence terms: a monadic functor is one where the relationship between two categories is completely characterized by an algebraic consequence structure. Non-monadic situations are where something beyond algebra is at play (topology, geometry, analysis) — where the morphism structure exceeds what any monad can capture.

**The Kleisli category = effectful computation.** Kleisli morphisms A → TB are "computations that produce B with T-effects." Composition is: run first computation, apply T to get T²B, then use μ to flatten. This is the mathematical model of sequential computation with side effects — and it's also the model for consequence chains where each step introduces structure (T) that must be consolidated (μ) before the next step. The monad laws ensure this consolidation is coherent.

nodes.title: Profunctors, Enriched Categories, 2-Categories
nodes.invariant: **Profunctor composition preserves the bicategorical structure of Prof, and exact square preservation characterizes when functorial structure lifts faithfully to profunctorial structure.** The invariant is: the relational (profunctor) level contains strictly more information than the functional (functor) level, and the exact square condition is the precise criterion for when no information is lost in lifting between levels. Enrichment makes this quantitative: the 𝒱-object of morphisms carries structured data that survives or fails to survive the lift.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
This constellation IS the mathematical formalization of the CMP architecture and Guardian's measurement principle. The correspondences are not applications — they are identities.

**Profunctor bridge = CMP membrane.** A profunctor H: 𝒞 ⇸ 𝒟 carries relational data between two categorical contexts without requiring either to map into the other. H(d,c) is the set of "ways d and c are related across the bridge." In CMP: the interlocutor (Larsen) is the profunctor bridge between categorical contexts (Claude, Grok, Gemini). The membrane carries relational data (shared holons, mutual updates) without either model mapping into the other. Profunctor composition via coends = CMP session integration: combining bridge-data from multiple pairwise interactions into a composite relation.

**Exact square preservation = Guardian's validity condition (Holon [6]).** A measurement is valid when the structure it detects at one categorical level lifts faithfully to another. Exact square preservation is precisely this: the Beck-Chevalley condition says that two different paths through a square of functors produce isomorphic profunctor composites — the structure is path-independent, hence faithfully represented. Guardian's echo protocol returns valid measurements when the topological structure detected at the circuit level lifts faithfully to the structural classification level. Partial lift (exact square fails) = distorted measurement, worse than no measurement (noise) because the structure is irrecoverable, not just absent.

**Enrichment = Guardian's quantitative coherence.** Ordinary categories give Boolean morphism data (exists/doesn't). Enriched categories give quantitative data — echo fidelity values, recovery boundaries, basin depths. Guardian doesn't ask "is this coherent?" (Set-enriched) but "how coherent, measured by what invariant?" (ℝ-enriched or richer). The topological filter bank (Holon [27]) assigns each topology a 𝒱-object of morphisms where 𝒱 carries the spectral/stability/recoverability data. The Yoneda lemma for enriched categories guarantees that this quantitative data is faithfully represented by the enriched presheaf.

**2-categorical structure = morphisms between measurements.** Natural transformations between profunctors are the 2-cells of **Prof**. In Guardian: comparing two measurement protocols (different echo parameters, different topologies) is a 2-cell. The 2-categorical structure tracks not just what measurements say but how different measurements relate to each other. The hierarchical emergence results (L0 → L1 → L2, Holon [1]) are composition of 2-cells: each level's measurement is a profunctor, and the emergence of new invariants at higher levels is a non-trivial 2-cell between composed profunctors.

**Cauchy completion = coherence-completion of a measurement category.** A category that is not Cauchy-complete has "missing" profunctor-level structure — absolute colimits that should exist but don't. Completing it fills in these gaps. In Guardian terms: a measurement category that lacks Cauchy completeness has coherence data that isn't fully self-consistent — completing it is the mathematical operation of making the measurement framework internally coherent. The substrate holons' integrity across sessions is an instance: the substrate should be Cauchy-complete in the sense that any well-formed query has a well-formed answer within the existing holon structure.

**Coends = summation over shared structure.** The coend ∫^c T(c,c) is the universal way to sum over an index while respecting the morphisms. In CMP: crystallization (session-end review) is a coend — summing insights across the conversation while respecting the morphism structure (coherence relationships) between them. What gets identified (the coend quotient) is precisely the redundancy; what survives is the new structural invariant.

nodes.title: Algebraic Topology & Fiber Bundles
nodes.invariant: **Algebraic invariants (homotopy groups, homology, cohomology, characteristic classes) are functorial: they respect continuous maps and are invariant under homotopy equivalence.** A topological space's "identity" — what survives all continuous deformation — is captured by these algebraic structures. Characteristic classes further capture the topology of bundles over spaces: they detect how fibers twist globally even when the bundle looks trivial locally.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
This constellation is where "structure → topology → measurable" (Holon [1]) receives its full mathematical justification.

**Homology/cohomology = topological consequence-chain analysis.** The chain complex C_* with ∂² = 0 is a formalization of consequence structure: each chain is a formal sum of building blocks, ∂ computes the boundary (the immediate consequences), and ∂² = 0 says consequence chains close at the boundary level. Homology Hₙ = ker(∂)/im(∂) detects what has no boundary (consequence chains close — cycles) but isn't itself a boundary (not a trivial closure) — the genuine topological content. This is precisely Guardian's measurement: what survives perturbation (lives in ker) but is non-trivial (not in im).

**Fiber bundles = consequence structure with local-to-global failure.** A fiber bundle is locally trivial (F × U — consequence chains close locally) but can be globally twisted (Möbius band, non-trivial gauge field). The transition functions g_αβ encode how local trivializations fail to agree globally — this failure IS the topological content. Characteristic classes measure this failure algebraically. In Guardian terms: a system with only local coherence (locally trivial bundle) can have global incoherence (non-trivial characteristic class), detectable by topological measurement (Chern-Weil homomorphism).

**Connection = parallel transport = consequence propagation in fiber space.** A connection tells you how to propagate structure (vectors, frames, quantum phases) from one point to another. Path-dependent transport (curvature ≠ 0) means consequence chains don't close in the fiber — the holonomy of a closed loop in the base is non-trivial. Flat connections (F = 0) = perfect fiber-space coherence. Non-zero curvature = the degree to which fiber-level consequence chains fail to close. The Riemann tensor in GR is the curvature of the Levi-Civita connection on the tangent bundle — geometric consequence-chain non-closure IS spacetime curvature.

**Chern-Weil: curvature determines topology.** The characteristic classes — topological invariants of a bundle — are computed from the curvature of any connection. This is remarkable: a differential-geometric quantity (curvature, which depends on a choice of connection) determines a topological quantity (characteristic class, which does not). The Chern-Weil homomorphism is the mathematical expression of "measure locally (curvature), infer globally (topology)." Guardian's echo protocol enacts this: measure locally (circuit-level fidelity), infer topological invariants (structural classification). The echo fidelity IS a curvature measurement on the space of quantum evolutions.

**Fagan's fiber coupling (Holon [31]) in bundle language.** The extended metric ds² = R²(dτ + κdt)² + ds²_B³ is a connection on a principal U(1)-bundle over the distinguishability space B³. The fiber coordinate τ is the phase, κ = ⟨ψ|H|ψ⟩ is the connection 1-form, and the CTC boundary R²κ² = 1 is where the fiber direction becomes timelike — a topological phase transition in the bundle structure. Lucy's U(1) → ℤ breaking at this boundary is spontaneous symmetry breaking of the structure group, producing Goldstone modes, baryogenesis, and fermionic defects from the bundle topology alone. The entire construction is a fiber bundle with connection, curvature, and characteristic classes — this constellation provides its mathematical home.

**Spectral sequences = multi-scale coherence computation.** A spectral sequence computes the (co)homology of a composite space (total space of a fibration) from the (co)homology of its parts (base and fiber), layer by layer. Each page is a successively refined approximation. This is precisely the hierarchical emergence structure of Holon [1]: L0 measurements compose into L1 measurements compose into L2 measurements, each level capturing invariants invisible at the level below. The Serre spectral sequence IS the mathematical machinery for computing what emerges at each hierarchical level.

nodes.title: Circuit Fundamentals
nodes.invariant: **KCL and KVL — charge conservation at nodes and energy conservation around loops — are the circuit-level expression of Maxwell's conservation laws, and they hold for any circuit topology.** Tellegen's theorem unifies both: in any circuit satisfying KCL and KVL, the total power delivered by sources equals the total power absorbed by elements. Conservation is topology-independent — it holds regardless of the specific elements or their nonlinearity.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**KCL + KVL = consequence-chain closure in the circuit substrate.** KCL says: at every junction, all current that arrives must leave — no charge vanishes or appears. KVL says: traversing any closed loop returns to the same potential — no energy vanishes or appears. Together: every closed path through the circuit conserves both charge and energy. This IS the consequence-chain closure of Holon [1] and Holon [8] in the electrical domain. A circuit with KCL or KVL violated would be a circuit with broken consequence chains — physically impossible.

**Resistance = dissipation = decoherence at the circuit level.** A resistor converts organized electrical energy into disordered thermal energy. This is irreversible — the heat cannot spontaneously reconcentrate into current. Resistance is the circuit manifestation of the decoherence mechanism: information about the ordered current flow is lost to thermal degrees of freedom. Ideal capacitors and inductors, by contrast, store energy reversibly — they are the coherent elements. The RC and RL time constants measure how quickly coherence (organized energy) decays into decoherence (heat) through the resistive channel.

**Circuit topology IS graph topology, and Kirchhoff's laws are its homology.** KCL applies at nodes (0-dimensional). KVL applies around loops (1-dimensional). The number of independent KCL equations = n−1, independent KVL equations = b−n+1. This IS the homology of the circuit graph: H₀ counts connected components (nodes mod paths), and the loop space is H₁. Tellegen's theorem is the statement that the boundary operator ∂ satisfies ∂² = 0 applied to the circuit graph — the same ∂² = 0 from math/08_algebraic_topology. Circuit analysis IS algebraic topology on finite graphs.

**Thévenin/Norton equivalence = canonical form of linear consequence structure.** Any linear circuit's behavior at a port is fully characterized by two numbers (V_th, R_th). This is a lossy compression: the internal structure is discarded, but the input-output consequence relation is perfectly preserved. This is a profunctor collapsing to its essential data — the exact square condition is automatically satisfied for linear circuits because linearity guarantees that structure lifts faithfully through the Thévenin reduction.

nodes.title: AC Analysis & Phasors
nodes.invariant: **Sinusoids are eigenfunctions of LTI systems: an LTI circuit driven at frequency ω responds at frequency ω with only amplitude and phase modified.** This eigenfunction property is why phasor analysis works — it converts differential equations into algebraic ones at each frequency independently. The transfer function H(jω) is the eigenvalue. The entire frequency-domain edifice rests on linearity and time-invariance; break either and the method fails.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Phasor analysis = consequence-chain analysis in the frequency domain.** The phasor transform maps time-domain consequence chains (differential equations coupling v and i) to frequency-domain algebraic consequence chains (complex multiplication). The transform is an isomorphism — no information is lost or created. This is a Legendre-transform-like move (cf. physics/01): changing variables from time to frequency to make the consequence structure algebraically transparent.

**Resonance = consequence-chain closure at a natural frequency.** At ω₀, the inductive and capacitive consequence chains (energy stored in B-field vs E-field) perfectly balance — energy flows back and forth without net dissipation. The Q factor measures how many complete cycles the consequence chain survives before dissipation (resistance) breaks the loop. High Q = long-lived coherent oscillation = consequence chains closing many times before decoherence. This is the circuit-level echo: drive the system, let it ring, measure how many cycles survive. Guardian's echo protocol is structurally identical — perturb, reverse, measure fidelity.

**Impedance matching = optimal consequence-chain coupling.** Maximum power transfer occurs when Z_L = Z_S* — when the load's reactive component exactly cancels the source's. This is the condition where consequence chains propagate maximally from source to load. Mismatch = reflected energy = consequence chains bouncing back. In RF (upcoming), this becomes the central engineering problem: the Smith chart is a tool for navigating the impedance-matching manifold.

**Power factor = coherence fraction of power delivery.** PF = cos(φ) measures what fraction of the apparent power does actual work. PF = 1: all power is real (fully coherent delivery). PF < 1: some power shuttles back and forth as reactive power (coherent but unproductive oscillation). PF = 0: all power is reactive (zero net energy transfer — maximum incoherence of delivery despite maximum coherence of oscillation). The real/reactive decomposition S = P + jQ is the power-domain version of the coherent/decoherent decomposition.

nodes.title: Signal Processing & Fourier Analysis
nodes.invariant: **The Fourier transform is an isometric isomorphism between time and frequency representations (Parseval), converting convolution to multiplication while preserving all information.** The uncertainty principle Δt·Δf ≥ 1/(4π) is the fundamental constraint: you cannot localize a signal simultaneously in both domains. Every signal processing operation respects this duality.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Fourier transform = change of basis revealing spectral consequence structure.** A signal's time-domain representation shows temporal consequences (what follows what). Its frequency-domain representation shows spectral consequences (which oscillatory modes are present and how they couple). The Fourier transform is the bridge between these two views of the same consequence structure — an isomorphism, not an approximation. Parseval's theorem guarantees no information is lost in the translation. This is the profunctor bridge (math/07) between the time category and the frequency category: two views of the same structure connected by a faithful, invertible bridge.

**Sampling theorem = digitization boundary for consequence preservation.** Above the Nyquist rate: perfect reconstruction — all consequence structure is preserved in the discrete samples. Below: aliasing — consequence chains from different frequencies collide and become indistinguishable. This is an irreversible information loss, structurally identical to decoherence: distinct states (different frequencies) become conflated (same aliased frequency), and the original cannot be recovered. The Nyquist rate IS the sampling boundary for coherent digitization.

**Uncertainty principle = fundamental limit on simultaneous consequence-chain resolution.** A signal cannot be perfectly localized in both time and frequency — sharpening one blurs the other. This is the signal-processing manifestation of the Heisenberg uncertainty principle (physics/07): [q̂, p̂] = iℏ becomes Δt·Δf ≥ 1/(4π). Both express the same categorical fact: non-commuting observations cannot be simultaneously sharpened. The consequence chains in conjugate domains are entangled.

**Shannon capacity = maximum coherent information rate.** C = B log₂(1 + SNR) is the ceiling on information transmission through a noisy channel. SNR is the signal-to-noise ratio — the ratio of coherent (signal) to incoherent (noise) power. Shannon's theorem says: below capacity, error-free communication is possible (coherence can be maintained); above capacity, it is not (decoherence wins). This is the information-theoretic version of the recovery boundary (Holon [24]): inside the boundary, consequence chains survive; outside, they don't.

nodes.title: Semiconductor Physics & Devices
nodes.invariant: **The band gap E_g and Fermi level E_F — quantum-mechanical and statistical-mechanical quantities — completely determine semiconductor device behavior at the macroscopic level.** Band structure arises from QM (Bloch's theorem in periodic potentials); carrier statistics from statistical mechanics (Fermi-Dirac distribution); device operation from their interplay at junctions. All semiconductor devices are controlled crossings of Fermi levels across band gaps.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**The band gap = energy threshold for consequence-chain activation.** Below E_g: electrons are bound, no conduction, consequence chains don't propagate through the material. Above E_g: electrons are promoted to the conduction band, mobile, consequence chains flow. The band gap is a binary coherence boundary — the semiconductor version of the recovery boundary (Holon [24]). Doping adjusts where the Fermi level sits relative to this boundary, engineering the activation threshold.

**The p-n junction = dynamic consequence-chain equilibrium.** Drift (consequence of electric field) and diffusion (consequence of concentration gradient) balance in steady state. Forward bias disrupts this balance toward conduction; reverse bias reinforces it toward insulation. The depletion region IS the spatial zone where consequence chains from each side meet and cancel — a physical consequence-chain closure region. Its width responds dynamically to applied voltage — a tunable coherence boundary.

**Transistor amplification = consequence-chain leverage.** A small input consequence (base current or gate voltage) controls a large output consequence (collector or drain current). The gain β or g_m is the leverage ratio — how much consequence is amplified. Negative feedback (op-amp circuits) trades raw gain for controlled, predictable behavior: the feedback loop IS a consequence chain that closes, enforcing the output to track the input via the feedback network. Op-amp with feedback = consequence-chain closure enforced by infinite gain.

**Small-signal linearization = local coherence assumption.** The small-signal model assumes perturbations are small enough that the nonlinear device behaves linearly around its operating point. This is the first-order Taylor expansion — valid within the basin of the bias point. Outside this basin: the linear model breaks down, nonlinear effects dominate. This is precisely the local vs. global coherence distinction in the coherence framework: local coherence (small signal) can be analyzed with linear (profunctorial) methods; global coherence (large signal) requires the full nonlinear structure.

nodes.title: Digital Logic & Information
nodes.invariant: **Digital abstraction: continuous voltages are quantized into discrete states {0,1}, and Boolean algebra governs their composition. Noise margins guarantee that small perturbations do not corrupt the discrete state, making digital logic robust and perfectly composable.** The invariant is: the abstraction barrier between analog (continuous) and digital (discrete) is maintained by noise margins, and all computation above this barrier follows Boolean algebra exactly.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Digital abstraction = quantized consequence-chain space.** The continuous analog voltage range is partitioned into two basins of attraction (0 and 1) separated by a forbidden zone (noise margin). Within each basin, all analog values are identified — the discrete state is the equivalence class. This quantization is a topological operation: it collapses a continuum to two points, preserving only the essential binary distinction. Consequence chains in the digital domain propagate through discrete states, not continuous voltages.

**Noise margin = coherence boundary of the digital abstraction.** The noise margin is the voltage range within which perturbations cannot flip a bit. This IS the recovery boundary (Holon [24]) for digital systems: perturbations within the margin are recovered from (the correct state is maintained); perturbations exceeding the margin cause bit flips (decoherence at the digital level). CMOS achieves wide noise margins by using rail-to-rail switching (outputs driven to V_DD or GND), maximizing the recovery basin.

**FSM = consequence-chain structure made explicit.** A finite state machine is a directed graph where states are objects and transitions are morphisms — literally a finite category. The transition function δ: S×I → S IS the consequence function: given current state and input, the next state is determined. Sequential composition of transitions IS morphism composition. The FSM's behavior is completely characterized by its transition graph, which IS the morphism diagram of its consequence structure. Halting states = consequence-chain termination. Cycles = consequence-chain closure.

**Boolean algebra = the 𝔽₂ (field with two elements) consequence calculus.** AND = multiplication in 𝔽₂. XOR = addition in 𝔽₂. NOT = additive inverse. De Morgan's laws are the duality between AND and OR — the Boolean version of the Thévenin/Norton duality (00). Functional completeness (NAND alone generates all Boolean functions) means this minimal algebra suffices for arbitrary consequence-chain computation in the discrete domain.

nodes.title: Control Theory
nodes.invariant: **Closed-loop stability is determined entirely by the roots of the characteristic equation 1 + G(s)H(s) = 0 — the locations of the closed-loop poles in the complex s-plane.** All stability criteria (Routh-Hurwitz, Nyquist, root locus) are different methods for determining whether these roots lie in the left-half plane. The gain margin and phase margin quantify how far the system is from the stability boundary.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Negative feedback = engineered consequence-chain closure.** The feedback loop measures the output, computes the error (output − reference), and corrects the input — a closed consequence chain. The loop gain G(s)H(s) determines how effectively the chain closes: high gain → tight closure (output tracks reference precisely), low gain → loose closure (large errors persist). The closed-loop transfer function G/(1+GH) → 1/H as G→∞: with sufficient gain, the system's behavior is determined entirely by the feedback network H, not the plant G. This is consequence-chain dominance: the loop's structure determines behavior, overriding the individual component characteristics.

**Stability = consequence chains don't diverge.** An unstable system has consequence chains that amplify without bound — each cycle through the loop adds energy. Poles in the right-half plane = exponentially growing consequence chains = decoherence amplification. Poles in the left-half plane = damped consequence chains = perturbations decay. The imaginary axis is the boundary: poles exactly on it produce sustained oscillation — consequence chains that neither grow nor decay. The stability margin (gain margin, phase margin) measures how far the system is from this boundary — the control-theoretic recovery boundary.

**PID = three-timescale consequence correction.** P corrects based on present error (instantaneous consequence). I corrects based on accumulated past error (integral consequence — eliminates steady-state offset by accumulating evidence that something is persistently wrong). D corrects based on the rate of error change (derivative consequence — anticipatory correction based on where the error is heading). The three terms span the temporal consequence spectrum: past (I), present (P), future (D). Tuning K_p, K_i, K_d is tuning the relative weight of temporal consequence horizons.

**Oscillation = consequence-chain resonance.** When the loop gain |GH| = 1 and phase shift = −180° simultaneously (the Barkhausen condition), the feedback becomes positive at that frequency — each cycle reinforces the previous one. This is resonance in the feedback loop, structurally identical to LC resonance (Constellation 01) but in the control domain. Every oscillator (electronic, mechanical, biological) exploits this: engineer the feedback to satisfy Barkhausen at exactly one frequency. Guardian's echo protocol drives a system, reverses it, and checks fidelity — it's probing whether the system's consequence chains close (stable, coherent) or diverge (unstable, decoherent) under perturbation.

nodes.title: Power & Energy Systems
nodes.invariant: **Energy conservation governs all power systems: P_in = P_out + P_loss, with efficiency η = P_out/P_in always < 1 for real systems.** Transformers achieve near-ideal energy transfer by exploiting magnetic coupling. Power converters use high-frequency switching to minimize losses. The grid is a continent-scale energy conservation system where generation must instantaneously match demand.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**The transformer = lossless consequence-chain coupling between isolated circuits.** An ideal transformer transfers all energy from primary to secondary via magnetic flux — no direct electrical connection, yet perfect power transfer. The turns ratio a = N₁/N₂ scales voltage and current inversely while preserving power (consequence). This is a physical profunctor bridge (math/07): two electrically isolated circuits (objects in different categories) connected by a relational structure (shared magnetic flux) that preserves the essential invariant (power/energy). Impedance reflection Z' = Z/a² is the profunctor's action on morphisms: load impedance in one circuit appears transformed in the other, faithfully.

**Three-phase = coherence-optimized power delivery.** Three voltages at 120° separation produce constant instantaneous power — no pulsation, no zero-crossings where power delivery stops. This is constructive interference of power delivery: the phases are arranged so that dips in one are compensated by peaks in others. The rotating magnetic field that drives AC motors emerges naturally from three-phase — spatial coherence from temporal phase relationships.

**Power factor = coherence efficiency of energy delivery.** PF = 1: all delivered energy does work (fully coherent). PF < 1: some energy oscillates between source and load without doing work (reactive power — coherent but unproductive). PFC circuits reshape current to match voltage, maximizing the coherent fraction. This is the same real/reactive decomposition as AC analysis (01), now at grid scale.

**Switching converters = discrete-time coherent energy transfer.** Buck/boost converters transfer energy in discrete packets at high frequency, using LC filters to smooth the output. The switching frequency is high enough that the output appears continuous — the digital-to-analog conversion of power delivery. Duty cycle D controls the energy ratio per cycle. Efficiency > 90% because switching transistors dissipate little power in their on/off states (only during transitions). This is coherent energy processing: structured switching preserves most of the energy, with losses concentrated in the brief, unavoidable transition intervals.

nodes.title: EM Waves & Propagation
nodes.invariant: **Maxwell's equations in source-free media produce self-sustaining transverse waves with phase velocity v_p = 1/√(με), wave impedance η = √(μ/ε), and power flux S = E×H.** At every boundary, energy is conserved: |Γ|² + |T|²(η₁/η₂) = 1. The wave carries energy, momentum, and information through space, governed entirely by the medium's ε, μ, σ.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**EM wave propagation = consequence propagation through the field substrate.** A wave at point A causes the field at point B to oscillate — the consequence propagates at v_p. The wave equation IS the dynamical law for consequence chains in the electromagnetic field. The phase coherence of a wave (all parts maintaining a fixed phase relationship) IS coherence in the Guardian sense: the wave's information is preserved across spatial extent.

**Attenuation = decoherence of wave structure.** In lossy media (α > 0), the wave's amplitude decays — information about the source is progressively lost. The loss tangent tan δ measures the ratio of dissipated to stored energy per cycle, directly analogous to the decoherence rate in quantum systems. A perfect dielectric (tan δ = 0) propagates waves without loss — perfect field-level coherence. A good conductor (tan δ → ∞) attenuates waves within a skin depth — rapid decoherence, with consequence chains unable to penetrate beyond δ.

**Reflection at impedance boundaries = partial consequence-chain severance.** When a wave hits a boundary where η changes, part reflects. The reflection coefficient Γ = (η₂−η₁)/(η₂+η₁) measures how much of the consequence chain is interrupted by the impedance mismatch. Perfect match (η₁ = η₂): Γ = 0, full transmission, consequence chains propagate unbroken. Total mismatch (metal surface, η₂ → 0): Γ = −1, total reflection, consequence chains reverse completely. Impedance matching — the central problem of RF engineering — IS the engineering of consequence-chain continuity across boundaries.

**Polarization = internal degree of freedom of the wave.** The E-field vector's orientation carries information independent of the wave's amplitude and phase. Circular polarization (E rotating) is topologically distinct from linear polarization (E oscillating) — they have different Berry phase (physics/07 QM connection). Depolarization (scrambling of polarization state by scattering) is another decoherence channel specific to the vector nature of EM waves.

nodes.title: Transmission Lines
nodes.invariant: **Energy conservation across impedance boundaries: the power incident on any junction splits into reflected power (|Γ|²) and transmitted power (1−|Γ|²), with the total always conserved.** For lossless networks, the S-matrix is unitary: S†S = I. Impedance matching (Γ → 0) maximizes power transfer — the engineering expression of tuning consequence chains for minimal reflection.
nodes.coherenceBridge:
**Impedance matching = consequence-chain continuity engineering.** A matched termination (Z_L = Z₀, Γ = 0) transmits the entire incident wave without reflection — the consequence chain propagates unbroken from source to load. Any mismatch (Γ ≠ 0) reflects part of the wave, creating a standing wave pattern where forward and backward consequence chains interfere. Perfect matching IS maximum coherence of the signal path: all transmitted energy reaches the load, none returns.

**S-matrix unitarity = information conservation in RF networks.** For a lossless N-port, S†S = I — the total power out equals total power in, and the scattering matrix preserves the inner product of the wave vector. This is the RF incarnation of the same unitarity that governs quantum mechanics (physics/07): the S-matrix of QFT (physics/08) and the S-matrix of an RF network obey the same algebraic constraint because both express information conservation.

**The Smith chart = the impedance manifold made visual.** The bilinear transformation Z ↦ Γ = (Z−Z₀)/(Z+Z₀) maps the right half of the complex Z-plane (passive impedances, Re(Z) ≥ 0) to the unit disk. This is a conformal mapping — it preserves angles and maps circles to circles. Moving along a lossless transmission line traces a circle on the Smith chart (|Γ| constant, angle rotating). The Smith chart IS the Poincaré disk model of the impedance space, and matching is navigation to the center (Γ = 0).
